scenes.name: Title
scenes.text: Structuring knowledge. [pause] Where understanding becomes memory.

scenes.name: Why
scenes.text: Understanding is necessary. It is not sufficient. [pause] A concept grasped on Monday will fade by Friday — unless it is structured, summarised in the student's own words, and revisited.

scenes.name: Techniques
scenes.text: Four techniques AI supports well. [pause] Summaries. Flashcards. Concept maps. Structured outlines. [pause] Each one organises raw understanding into a stable mental structure.

scenes.name: Principle
scenes.text: But here is the rule. [pause] If AI generates the summary — the student has a summary. [pause] If the student creates it and AI checks it — the student has both a summary <emphasis>and</emphasis> deeper understanding.

scenes.name: Workflow
scenes.text: The workflow is three steps. [pause] Attempt. Check. Revise. [pause] The student writes first. AI evaluates. The student rewrites — better.

scenes.name: Flashcards
scenes.text: Flashcards are a perfect example. [pause] AI can generate twenty cards in seconds. [pause] But the student who decides what is worth testing learns more than the student who just receives the deck.

scenes.name: Cycle
scenes.text: Knowledge structuring is not a one-time event. [pause] As new material arrives, structures expand. Outlines reorganise. Flashcard sets grow. [pause] Revisiting is where retention lives.

scenes.name: Classroom
scenes.text: In your classroom — end every lesson with a five-sentence student summary. [pause] Then let AI flag what was missed. [pause] Small habit. Big difference.
